chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Hi, the next topic is asymptotic notation and this is a very important topic, very important

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: topic in algorithm.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us understand what this topic is about.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So first of all let me tell you that this topic is coming from mathematics.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Functions belongs to mathematics so this topic also belongs to mathematics.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But as for the time complexity we are using functions in algorithms that is the reason

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this is also used, the notations are also used.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The notations are used for representing the simple form of a function or showing the class

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: of a function.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If we have any function we can show that this function belongs to so and so class and we

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: already know the comparison of functions also which function is smaller and which is greater

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: than which one so we know their order, increasing order also.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now for representing any function in simple communicable form so that one can easily get

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the idea about the algorithm so that is why this is the mostly needed topic for algorithms

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: because we cannot write a lengthy function every time.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We need a simple method for representing the time complexity.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: What are the notations used?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Big O notation, this works as an upper bound of a function.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Big Omega notation, this works as a lower bound of a function.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And Theta notation, this works as an average bound of a function.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Any function you can represent as either upper bound or lower bound or average bound.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now what is useful out of these three?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This one is useful.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then why do you have these two?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Why do you need upper bound?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you cannot specify the exact place of any function out of these then you can show the

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: upper bound of that function.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now let me tell you one thing, when you find the time complexity of any algorithm, the

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: time complexity will be one among these.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And if it is not among these, it may be a multiple of these.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If it is not even multiple of these, then you may not be able to exactly represent Theta

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then you can go for Big O or Omega.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So anyway we will see through these examples.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is a Big O notation.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: As per the definition, the definition says that the function f of n is equal to Big O

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: of g of n if and only if there exist positive constants c and n0 such that f of n is less

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: than equal to c into g of n for all n greater than n0 or n and not.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is the definition.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now what does it mean?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us understand.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For example, if I have a function f of n is equal to 2n plus 3, then 2n plus 3 is less

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: than equal to something I should write here such that that is greater than or equal to

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this one.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So what I can write?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I can write anything that should be greater than or equal to this one.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But one thing I have to take care.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This function is having two terms.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So I should not write multiple terms.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I should write only a single term.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That single term can have some coefficient.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That c means it can have some coefficient.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So what I do is I write 10n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Is it greater?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Yes, this is greater than this one.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I put 1 here, this is going to be 10.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I put 1 here, this is going to be 5.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So 10 is greater than 5.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is for all n greater than or equal to 1 onwards.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So in this, this is f of n, this is c and this is g of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So g of n is what?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now therefore I can say f of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: What is that f of n?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 2n plus 3 is big O of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: g of n is n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So f of n is big O of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See I have written 10n there.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So 10n is what I should write.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So students get confused here.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then whatever you want you can write there.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But make sure that this portion is greater than this one.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Or this function is greater than this one.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So can I write 7n here also?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Yes.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So 7 is also true and 10n is also true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 1000n also is true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Anything you write there but make sure that this is greater than that one for some starting value of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now instead of going through all this, instead of struggling to get the value there,

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: so the simple method is you can make this as 2n plus, this is just 3, this also you make it as 3n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this will be 2n plus 3 is less than 5n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And this will be true for all values greater than equal to 1.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I put 1 there, both the sides will be 5, 5 they are equal.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I put any other greater value of n, then definitely 5n will be greater.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that's it.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Again this is g of n and this is true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: f of n is big O of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: f of n is big O of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So a simple method to get this side function is make all these terms as multiples of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For the same function, can I write 2n square plus 3n square?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And that becomes 2n plus 3 is less than equal to 5n square for all values of n greater than equal to 1.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Yes, this is also true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is also true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then what is g of n here?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is c and this is g of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And as we know that's f of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So what is g of n we got? n square.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So can we say that this function, this same function, can it be big O of n square?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Yes, this is also true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is also true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Whether you say big O of n, big O of n square, that is also true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: As we are writing an upper bound, that is big O, that is upper bound, so both n and n square becomes upper bound of that function.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This f of n actually belongs to this class.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It belongs to this type of function.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This one.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It means all those functions which are greater than or equal to this one, including this, this becomes upper bound.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And all these functions, including this one, on this side becomes lower bound.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And what this exactly becomes?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This becomes average bound.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Average bound.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That's it.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: All those functions I can write them as upper bound.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So saying for this function, big O of n is also true, big O of n square is also true, f of n is equal to big O of 2 power n is also true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: All those are true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But I cannot say f of n is big O of log n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: No.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is coming on this side.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is coming on lower bound side.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So I cannot say it is big O of log n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This will be wrong.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is wrong.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I can say all these things.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: All these are true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now the question arises, why do I need to write all those when it is actually equal to this one?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Yes.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: When you write big O, try to write the closest function.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Try to use the closest function.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So which is the closest function in this one?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is the closest function.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Though if you write any other higher value function also, it is true, it is not false.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But it is not useful.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we prefer writing anything that is closer to that one.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Right.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is about big O notations.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Omega notation.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: As per the definition, the function f of n is omega g of n if and only if there exist positive constants c and n0 such that f of n is greater than or equal to c into g of n for all n greater than or equal to n0.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is the definition.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Compared to big O notation, only the symbol has changed and this symbol has changed.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The inequality sign has changed.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This was less than equal to, now it is greater than equal to.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That is the only difference.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now if I take the same example function, f of n is equal to 2n plus 3, then I can write 2n plus 3 is greater than equal to just 1n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So earlier we saw in big O we were writing every term as n, now you can just write 1n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: You can take the help of 1 here.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So just 1n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For all n greater than or equal to 1.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Right.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Even maybe 0, it is starting from 0 also.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that is not much important.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Anyway, you can find out from which value it is starting, you can write on that one.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So 2n plus 3 is greater than or equal to 1n for all n starting from 0 onwards or 1 onwards.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is f of n and this is c and this is g of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So therefore I can say f of n is omega of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So can I say here instead of 1, can I say log n?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Yes, this function is greater than log n also.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So even I can say f of n is omega of log n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is also true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is also true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But I cannot use any value other than this one.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if you remember that this belongs to this class.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So I cannot write anything beyond this one.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So I cannot say f of n is big omega of n square.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: No, this will be wrong.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This will be wrong because this is becoming an upper bound, this is not a lower bound.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So I can write anything from this side.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: All will be true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Anything you write that will be true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Either you write log of log n or root n or log n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Anything you write that is true.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But which one is useful?

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The nearest one is useful.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This one.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Because this directly belongs to this class.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is equal.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Greater or this is less than or equal I can say.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The function is greater than or equal to this one.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is less than or equal to the function.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Alright.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if I write any other smaller value, that may be true but not useful.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Next one is theta notation.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: As per the definition, the function f of n is theta of g of n if and only if there exists positive constants c1, c2 and n0 or n0 such that c1 into g of n is less than equal to f of n and that is less than equal to c2 into g of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I take the same example, f of n is equal to 2n plus 3.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then 2n plus 3.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Already we found the lower bound that was 1 into n and already we found the upper bound that was 5 into n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is f of n and this is acting as c1 and this is g of n.

chunks.title: Asymptotic Notations Big Oh - Omega - Theta #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is c2 and this is g of n.

chunks.text: So both the sides we have g of n as n and it is same.

chunks.text: These are just notation for representing bounds of a function.
